parsed_review.summary: CP00-609 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (65 RP19 units, RP19.P09.M06.S08-RP19.P09.M10.S04, risk C) completing the RP19.P09 review-evidence/closeout bridge and handing off to RP20.P00.M00.S01 (CP00-610). The pack correctly claims production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status in_progress, implementation_layer descriptor — no over-claim of production readiness or enterprise trust, consistent with the standing directive never to weaken production_ready gates. Verified: all 7 required public exports exist (createClientPortalCoreCp609P09ReviewEvidenceCloseoutBridgeDescriptor/CaseSet in service.js; validateClientPortalCoreCp609Coverage, the bridge descriptor validator, and the Hermes/Claude/Closeout packet builders in validators.js) and are structured descriptor-only. Coverage counts (implementation 40 / claude_review 11 / security_audit 8 / test 3 / ui 3 = 65; micro-phases M06=13/M07=20/M08=20/M09=8/M10=4) are consistent across review-baseline, registry CP609_REQUIREMENTS, validators, the 3 model.test.js tests, the contract projection, and the manifest. Tests draw the plan pack from the live closeout-pack-plan.json (not a hardcoded fixture) and assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries; scripts/validate-rp19-client-portal-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the contract projection (cp609_requirements, cp609_no_write_attestation, p09_review_evidence_closeout_bridge_descriptor) against code and preserves all prior packs' coverage (CP583-CP609). No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp609Result forces every runtime/write/storage/audit/hermes-receipt flag false and no_real_data/synthetic_only true. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob; claude_is_final_approval=false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). Hermes patterns are confined to the deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening guards (forbidden_review_evidence) enumerate auth failure, zero-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing raw-output/source-inspection basis, and final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte stdout) with no preserved invalid attempts and prompt 2274 bytes (no full diff/source). Upstream/downstream chain is intact (CP608.next=CP609; CP609.upstream=CP608; CP609.first_unit=CP608.next_subphase=RP19.P09.M06.S08; CP609.next=CP610=RP20.P00.M00.S01). Unrelated dirty files (UI progress-control-room, workbook/* docs, the ldip invalid-attempt JSON, the docx) are correctly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and were not treated as in-scope. Pre-review command-evidence shows all 17 node --check/--test, rp19/weighted/spec/rp00/rp05/goal/fullplan validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, and git diff --check commands at exit 0; plan-level closeout-pack-plan:validate / closeout-pack:validate and the post-review adjudication, construction-inspection, and canonical receipt are correctly deferred to the hardened post-review sequence (gate_outcome pending_post_review_pack_evidence_creation), which is the expected state at review time, not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; nothing in this review blocks pack or goal closeout.
